scope: apps/web/src/App.tsx monthly contribution UI and authenticated API calls
apps/web/src/monthly-contribution.ts helper contracts for cycle month, strategy, status, amount validation, report gating, API base normalization, and currency formatting

apps/web/src/App.test.tsx executable helper assertions, including local timezone cycle month, invalid amount rejection, terminal cycle gating, report gating, invalid currency, and API base normalization
apps/web/src/styles.css responsive layout, validation state, and visible focus states
apps/api/src/reports POST body contract, selected contribution cycle report binding, and duplicate selected-cycle report rejection
apps/api/src/reports and apps/api/src/contribution-cycles HTTP regression tests for selected-cycle report generation and terminal cycle reopening

deterministic collector signals for frontend behavior, backend boundary changes, portfolio-scoped list loading, local Headers construction, DTO validation, i18n, magic result strings, and large component size
residualRisk: Approved with notes after fix loop: external review found three actionable issues around terminal cycle confirmation, stale or duplicate report state, and invalid confirmed amounts. The fixes add frontend amount normalization, UI report gating by selected cycle, API report binding to contributionCycleId, transactional CONFIRMED -> REPORTED transition before saved report creation, duplicate selected-cycle report rejection, server-side terminal-cycle reopening protection, and canonical repository-service result statuses for the new report contract. Independent reviewer initially requested backend enforcement for duplicate reports and terminal status transitions; the second review found no blockers. Remaining accepted risks: POST /reports without contributionCycleId intentionally remains a backward-compatible generic saved-report path, strategy/status contracts are mirrored in the web helper instead of shared package imports, portfolio-scoped lists are still loaded without a new pagination contract, App.tsx remains a large component, and formal OpenAPI/i18n extraction is still absent. No financial advice, order flow, or broker automation was introduced.
